data.title: Linnovate, alternative investment asset services provider, introduces new brand identity after 10 years of growth and innovation.
data.description: Today, on January 26, 2026, in Singapore, Linnovate, a worldwide company offering comprehensive asset services for the alternative investment sector, revealed its updated brand identity. This rebranding marks a significant milestone for the company, symbolizing ten years of expansion, creativity, and strategic development. Formerly recognized as Linnovate, the company is now portraying a new image to the world.

data.title: Elbit Systems gets $228M contract for Iron Fist APS for US Army Bradley IFVs.
data.description: On January 26, 2026, in Haifa, Israel, Elbit Systems Ltd. declared the US Government's recent award on September 29, 2025, to General Dynamics Ordnance and Tactical Systems (GD-OTS). Elbit Systems, listed on NASDAQ as ESLT and TASE as ESLT, made this announcement.

data.title: TotalEnergies extends Waha Concessions in Libya until 2050, aiming to increase production from current 370,000 barrels.
data.description: During the recent Libya Energy & Economy Summit in Tripoli, Patrick Pouyanné, TotalEnergies' Chairman and CEO, signed a deal on January 24th extending the Waha Concessions until December 31, 2050. This agreement was signed in the presence of Abdul Hamid Dbeiba, the Prime Minister of the Government of National Unity. The agreement introduces revised fiscal terms aimed at boosting production levels from these concessions, which are presently yielding about 370,000 barrels.

data.title: Brown & Brown, Inc. (NYSE: BRO) gearing up for Q4 print, analysts project 90 cents/share earnings, $1.65 billion revenue.
data.description: Brown & Brown, Inc. will announce its third-quarter earnings after the market closes on Jan. 26. Analysts are predicting earnings of 90 cents per share, an increase from the 86 cents per share reported in the same period last year. The consensus estimate for the company's quarterly revenue is $1.65 billion, compared to $1.18 billion in the previous year. The company recently declared a regular quarterly cash dividend of 16.5 cents per share. Closing at $79.18 on Friday, the company's shares saw a 0.7% increase. For more information, including analyst ratings and stock information, readers can visit Benzinga.com.

data.title: Week 4: Various stock exchanges and stakeholders involved in share buyback program, monitored by Danish Financial Supervisory Authority.
data.description: The stock exchanges of Nasdaq Copenhagen, Euronext Dublin, and the London Stock Exchange, along with the Danish Financial Supervisory Authority and various other stakeholders, are involved in a collaborative effort. This initiative aims to enhance transparency and regulatory compliance within the financial industry. The project involves key players from the mentioned exchanges and regulatory bodies, showcasing cooperation among institutions.

data.title: Steel Dynamics may report earnings at $1.69/share on revenue of $4.57B. Baker Hughes Co. posted earnings of 78 cents/share, beating estimates.
data.description: U.S. stock futures are down this morning, and investors are eyeing certain stocks today. Wall Street predicts Steel Dynamics Inc. (NASDAQ: STLD) will announce quarterly earnings of $1.69 per share on revenue amounting to $4.57 billion. Steel Dynamics shares climbed 1.4% to $181.32 at Friday's close. Meanwhile, Baker Hughes Co. (NASDAQ: BKR) reported positive fourth-quarter results, with adjusted earnings of 78 cents per share surpassing market expectations. For more details, visit Benzinga.com.
